@slavicemperor8279 Now this is something coming from someone who knows nothing about Gorr: This guy was not menacing at all honestly. The only thing that had me worried was the fact that he had the necrosword. He kidnapped kids ffs. Thats the only thing I will remember him for. He kidnaped kids and tormented them for absolutely NO reason. Feels like something Rita Repulsa from Power Rangers or some kid friendly Disney villain would do. I understand why he kidnapped them but he had nothing to gain for tormenting those kids and it felt out of character honestly for someone who had a daughter and loved her dearly. And to make things worse, everyone watching that obviously knew ABSOLUTELY NOTHING bad was gonna happen to those kids. Kidnapping is literally the worst the story could do to them honestly. It's not like we were watching Game of Thrones. So it had no impact at all. It was a terrible idea to use that as a way to show how menacing he was.
They had the opportunity to show how powerful he was by showing him overpower some gods who's strength we could kinda "estimate" maybe to be on the same level as Thor or maybe even seemingly superior.
At no point did I feel like he was stronger than Thor. At no point was I scared for Thor. He just happened to have a sword that can kill gods. Gods who happen to have weapons that can probably kill him...🙄🙄 Don't even get me started with those shadow monsters.

The whole armour is UGLY and looks like a one piece cosplay costume that you just wear over your head or zip at the back. ALL the costumes were ugly! Jane and Gorr dies

Thor parts ways with the Guardians of the Galaxy after receiving a distress signal from Sif. Upon arrival, a beaten Sif warns Thor of Gorr, a being possessing the god-killing weapon, the Necrosword, seeking the extinction of all gods as revenge for ignoring the death of his family and that his next target is New Asgard.

Dr. Jane Foster, Thor's ex-girlfriend, arrives at New Asgard in hopes of seeking medical treatment for her cancer. When Gorr attacks New Asgard, Thor's old hammer Mjolnir reforges and bonds itself to Foster after sensing her worthiness - granting her the power of Thor. Thor eventually arrives in New Asgard and is surprised to find Foster with Mjolnir. Thor teams up with Valkyrie, Korg and Foster to fight Gorr and his shadow creatures. The group is able to thwart Gorr but he escapes, kidnapping several Asgardian children.

The group travels to Omnipotent City to warn the other gods and ask for their help. The Olympian god Zeus is unwilling to help and has Thor captured, forcing the group to fight off Zeus's men. Zeus heavily injures Korg; in anger, Thor appears to have killed Zeus with his own thunderbolt, which Valkyrie steals during their escape. They then travel to the Shadow Realm to save the children. However, this turned out to be a ruse for Gorr to take Stormbreaker, which he intends to use on the Bifrost to enter Eternity and ask for the destruction of all gods. Gorr manages to overpower Thor's group and successfully steal Stormbreaker. Gorr uses Stormbreaker to open the portal to Eternity. With Valkyrie and Foster injured, Thor imbues the kidnapped Asgardian children and their weapons with the power of Thor to fight alongside him. Foster joins Thor in fighting Gorr and destroys the Necrosword.

Admitting defeat, Thor manages to convince Gorr that all he wanted from Eternity was not to destroy the gods, but to get his daughter Love back. Foster succumbs to her illness and dies in Thor's arms. Eternity allows Gorr's request to revive Love, whom he asks Thor to take care of before he dies from the effects of the Necrosword. The children return to New Asgard, where Valkyrie and Sif begin training them. Meanwhile, Thor, now in possession of Mjolnir again, continues to go on adventures to help people, with Love, now wielding Stormbreaker, by his side.

In a mid-credits scene, the injured Zeus sends his son Hercules to kill Thor. In a post-credit scene, Foster arrives at the gates of Valhalla, where Heimdall welcomes her.

Thor parts ways with the Guardians of the Galaxy after receiving a distress signal from Sif. Upon arrival, a beaten Sif warns Thor of Gorr, a being possessing the god-killing weapon the Necrosword seeking the extinction of all gods as revenge for ignoring the death of his family, and that his next target is New Asgard.
Dr. Jane Foster, Thor's ex-girlfriend, arrives at New Asgard in hopes of seeking medical treatment for her cancer. When Gorr attacks New Asgard, Thor's old hammer Mjolnir reforges and bonds itself to Foster after sensing her worthiness - granting her the power of Thor. Thor eventually arrives in New Asgard and is surprised to find Foster with Mjolnir. Thor teams up with Valkyrie, Korg and Foster to fight Gorr and his shadow creatures. The group is able to thwart Gorr but he escapes, kidnapping several Asgardian children.
The group travels to Omnipotent City to warn the other gods and ask for their help. The Olympian god Zeus is unwilling to help and has Thor captured, forcing the group to fight off Zeus's men. Zeus heavily injures Korg; in anger, Thor wounds Zeus with his own thunderbolt, which Valkyrie steals during their escape. They then travel to the Shadow Realm to save the children. However, this turned out to be a ruse for Gorr to take Stormbreaker, which he intends to use it on the Bifrost to enter Eternity and ask for the destruction of all gods. Gorr manages to overpower Thor's group and successfully steal Stormbreaker. Gorr uses Stormbreaker to open a portal to Eternity. With Valkyrie and Foster injured, Thor imbues the kidnapped Asgardian children and their weapons with the power of Thor to fight alongside him. Foster joins Thor in fighting Gorr and destroys the Necrosword.
Admitting defeat, Thor manages to convince Gorr that all he wanted from Eternity was not to destroy the gods, but to get his daughter Love back. Foster succumbs to her illness and dies in Thor's arms. Eternity allows Gorr's request to revive Love, whom he asks Thor to take care of before he dies from the effects of the Necrosword. The children return to Asgard, where Valkyrie and Sif begin training them. Meanwhile, Thor, now in possession of Mjolnir again, continues to go on adventures to help people, with Love, now wielding Stormbreaker, by his side.
In a mid-credits scene, the injured Zeus sends his son Hercules to kill Thor. In a post-credit scene, Foster arrives at the gates of Valhalla, where Heimdall welcomes her
